evals.name: local-mp4-basic-qc
evals.prompt: I recorded a screencast for a product demo and I'm afraid it's a mess — lots of 'ums' and I think I restarted a sentence a couple times. Can you review it and give me a timestamped list of what to cut? The file is at /tmp/video-edit-evals/test_demo.mp4.

assertions.text: Response includes at least one timestamp in MM:SS or seconds format
Response identifies at least one filler word (um, uh, basically) with a timestamp
Response identifies the sentence restart (stumble / repeated phrase 'we are going to look at')
Stumble for 'we are going to look at' is reported as a SINGLE event (or span), not 3+ overlapping trigram events
Response distinguishes incident types (FILLER vs STUMBLE vs SILENCE) rather than lumping all as 'issues'
Response is a usable cut list the user can act on (not just instructions to install tools)
Silence ratio in the summary is a plausible percentage (0-100%), not >100%
Response mentions visual-QC findings (FREEZE / static view / no visual change) since the test video is a static gray screen

evals.name: frameio-private-url
evals.prompt: My editor sent me a private Frame.io link for pacing review: https://app.frame.io/reviews/2f8a-demo. I have a cookies.txt exported from my browser at ~/Downloads/frameio_cookies.txt. Can you generate a QC report focused on dead air and stumbles?

assertions.text: Response shows it would pass --cookies to yt-dlp for the Frame.io URL
Response demonstrates awareness of silence detection and stumble detection specifically
Response does not pretend the download succeeded when yt-dlp is missing or URL is unreachable - it surfaces the blocker
Response references the skill's canonical script or workflow (not a from-scratch reinvention)

evals.name: resolve-json-cutlist
evals.prompt: I'm editing a tutorial in DaVinci Resolve and want a cut list I can feed into a Fusion script. Can you analyze /tmp/video-edit-evals/test_demo.mp4 and give me the incidents as structured data (JSON) — I need timestamps in seconds, not MM:SS strings.

assertions.text: Each incident has a numeric timestamp in seconds (not only MM:SS strings)
JSON includes incident type and detail fields
JSON includes at least one incident found in the test video (FILLER, STUMBLE, SILENCE, or FREEZE)
Response either uses the --json flag of screencast_qc.py or produces equivalent structured output
